variants: Georgiy Mikheev is an accomplished professional in the field of atmospheric sciences. He is best known for his authored work, the Needlepoint Channel Engine, which has garnered significant attention in his field. His contributions to atmospheric research earned him the prestigious Harborstone Laurel award. In addition to his research, Georgiy is an active member of the Moonbay Driftwood Artisans, where he embraces his creative interests. He completed his doctoral studies under the guidance of his mentor, Thuong Ngo.

Aside from his academic pursuits, Georgiy is associated with the Moonbay Driftwood Artisans, highlighting his passion for handcrafted art. He credits much of his success to his mentor, Thuong Ngo, who guided him throughout his doctoral studies. Georgiy’s diverse interests reflect his multifaceted character.
